description: The Gulf Cartel is a Mexican drug trafficking organization based in Matamoros, Tamaulipas, founded in the 1930s by Juan Nepomuceno Guerra. Initially smuggling alcohol during Prohibition, it later expanded into cocaine, methamphetamine, and marijuana under Juan García Ábrego. It is one of the oldest organized crime groups in Mexico and is designated as a terrorist organization by the United States. The cartel is known for its violence and international network.

country_links.evidence_quote: The U.S. federal court awarded two helicopters owned by Cárdenas to the Business Development Bank of Canada and the GE Canada Equipment Financing respectively.
country_links.context: The Gulf Cartel's assets, including helicopters bought with drug proceeds, were awarded to Canadian entities.

country_links.evidence_quote: By negotiating with the Cali Cartel, García Ábrego was able to secure 50% of the shipment out of Colombia as payment for delivery
country_links.context: The Gulf Cartel sourced cocaine from Colombia via the Cali Cartel.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Gulf Cartel is believed to have ties with the 'Ndrangheta, an organized crime group in Italy that also has ties with Los Zetas.
country_links.context: The Gulf Cartel has ties with the 'Ndrangheta organized crime group in Italy.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Gulf Cartel, along with their rival group Los Zetas, have been the two drug cartels with the most kidnappings in all of Mexico... In the United States, the Gulf Cartel has been responsible for several kidnappings, primarily in the McAllen metropolitan area.
country_links.context: The Gulf Cartel smuggles drugs into the United States, extorts businesses there, and launders money through U.S. bank accounts.

country_links.evidence_quote: Mexican organizations, such as the Gulf Cartel, are currently exploiting West Africa's struggling rule-of-law... to stage and expand supply routes to the increasingly lucrative European illegal drug market.
country_links.context: The Gulf Cartel is active in West Africa, using the region to stage supply routes to Europe.
